workContext.summary: The user is associated with 潭州书院 (Tanzhou Academy) at 湘潭理工 (Xiangtan University of Technology), involved in designing and evaluating training programs for new 辅导员 (counselors/instructors). They are currently analyzing a 2026 summer flipped classroom training program called '新辅导员入职研修：在爱里，生长热爱', a 7-day workshop for 20-35 new counselors, scheduled June 30 to July 6, 2026, co-organized with 彩虹之桥. The user has revealed deeper layers of the training philosophy, rooted in Buddhist concepts of '缘起' (causes/conditions) and '执取' (attachment/clinging), and has disclosed the use of AI and 20+ years of organizational development practice to productize '发现/发展喜欢与天赋' (discovering/developing passion and talent).

personalContext.summary: The user communicates in Chinese and has a critical, detail-oriented approach. They value deep, honest, and nuanced analysis over superficial praise, and engage with educational content by asking probing questions about its effectiveness and impact on participants. The user is philosophically and psychologically sophisticated, grounding their training design in Buddhist epistemology (缘起/执取), and they explicitly value the integration of ancient wisdom with modern AI/tech tools for self-cognition.

topOfMind.summary: The user is currently focused on critically evaluating the 2026潭州书院暑期翻转课堂研修营 training program for new辅导员. They have now revealed the deeper philosophical and technological foundation: the training is built on a Buddhist-derived framework where each person's unique '出厂设置' (factory settings) leads to different '缘起' (perceived conditions) and '执取' (instinctive attachments), and AI-powered assessments (336-item self-assessment, 112-element trait profiling) serve as modern tools for '观照' (clear seeing) of one's own nature. The user has productized this into three key intervention points: '科学选专业' (scientific major selection), '科学学习' (scientific learning), and '科学发展' (scientific development). The conversation has reached a meta-level, where the user is testing whether the assistant can truly grasp and articulate this framework's depth. The user's core question remains: can 7 days genuinely change cognition and translate into action, but now examined through the lens of whether the Buddhist-derived framework of 'seeing one's缘起' through AI tools can achieve what traditional introspection alone cannot.

recentMonths.summary: The user engaged in a detailed roleplay scenario asking the assistant to act as a 导员 (instructor/counselor) to analyze training content. After the assistant initially produced a fabricated sports analysis based on OCR hallucination from a black image, the user corrected this error. The user then provided the actual content: a detailed 2026 summer flipped classroom training program for new counselors at 潭州书院 × 彩虹之桥, Xiangtan University of Technology, featuring a 7-day workshop with 336-item self-assessment, 112-element trait profiling, and nine core responsibilities. The user then challenged the assistant to evaluate whether this program can truly '点亮' (light up) counselors, leading to an in-depth discussion about participant personas and program effectiveness. The conversation then deepened further with the user asking whether 7 days can genuinely change cognition and translate into action, which prompted the assistant to ground the analysis in psychological theories. Finally, the user revealed the deeper philosophical and technological framework: the training is built on Buddhist concepts of 缘起 and 执取, combined with AI-powered self-assessment tools, representing 20+ years of organizational development practice productized into '科学选专业/科学学习/科学发展'.The assistant admitted it lacked direct image-reading tools and reiterated its no-hallucination policy from the previous OCR error, asking the user to describe the image content instead.

facts.content: The user explicitly corrected the assistant when the assistant fabricated sports training analysis from OCR hallucination on a black image. The user pointed out 'this image has nothing to do with sports.'
facts.sourceError: The assistant blindly trusted OCR output (Tesseract hallucination) that generated fake text about bench press training from a nearly pure black PNG image (99.9% black pixels), leading to a detailed but completely wrong analysis.

facts.content: The assistant conducted pixel-level analysis revealing the uploaded image was a 1080x1366 PNG with 99.9% pure black pixels and no text, graphics, or design elements.
The user is associated with 潭州书院 (Tanzhou Academy) and 湘潭理工 (Xiangtan University of Technology), involved in training programs for new 辅导员 (counselors/instructors).

The user provided a detailed training program titled '2026 · 潭州书院 · 暑期翻转课堂研修营: 新辅导员入职研修：在爱里，生长热爱' which is a 7-day flipped classroom training for 20-35 new counselors, scheduled June 30 to July 6, 2026.

The training program is built around three layers: the nine core responsibilities of counselors (底层), flipped classroom based on personal trait profiling (中层), and CFAP literacy improvement (目标: 品格力量, 基础能力, 行动力, 专业能力).
The user values deep, critical analysis of educational/training content over superficial summaries, and expects the assistant to engage with the material's design philosophy and pedagogical approach.
The user corrected the assistant's previous error of blindly trusting OCR hallucination (sports analysis from a black image), expecting independent visual verification before drawing conclusions.

The user challenged the assistant to evaluate whether the 2026潭州书院暑期翻转课堂研修营 training program can genuinely '点亮' (light up) counselors, demonstrating a critical and analytical approach to educational program assessment.

The user engaged the assistant in a nuanced discussion about three types of counselors who might respond differently to the training: (1) those with passion who get lit up, (2) experienced counselors who may find it too slow, (3) those treating it as a transitional job who cannot be reached, and (4) those who initially underestimate the role but could be transformed by the program.

The user values honest and critical analysis that considers different participant personas and potential limitations of training designs, rather than just surface-level praise or descriptive summaries.

The training program is co-organized by 潭州书院 and 彩虹之桥 at 湘潭理工, scheduled June 30 to July 6, 2026, targeting 20-35 new辅导员, featuring a 336-item self-assessment and 112-element trait profiling approach.

The training program's daily structure includes morning classic reading of 《大学》, morning sessions focused on nine core responsibilities with expanding interaction circles (self→partners→cross-group→cross-team→cross-school→boundless), afternoon real-world visits (factory tours, historical sites, enterprise connections), and evening short video discussions.

The user challenged the assistant to ground their analysis in established psychological theories, specifically asking whether 7 days can change cognition and translate into action. The assistant referenced Prochaska's behavior change model, Mezirow's transformative learning theory, and habit formation research (Lally et al., 2009) to distinguish between what short-term training can achieve (planting 'cognitive seeds') versus what requires longer follow-up.

The user distinguishes between four types of counselor personas: (1) those with existing passion who get lit up, (2) experienced counselors who may find the program too slow, (3) those treating the role as a transitional job who cannot be reached, and (4) those who initially underestimate the role's potential but could be transformed by the program.

The user values analysis that distinguishes between different levels of cognitive change: seeing something new, developing cognitive flexibility, forming specific intentions, building automated habits, and reconstructing core identity. They expect the assistant to be precise about which levels a 7-day program can realistically achieve.

The training program features a daily structure with morning classic reading of 《大学》, morning sessions on nine core responsibilities with expanding interaction circles (self→partners→cross-group→cross-team→cross-school→boundless), afternoon real-world visits, and evening short video discussions. The program includes specific locations: 吉利工厂 (Geely factory), 曾国藩故居 (Zeng Guofan's former residence), and self-selected options like 橘子洲 (Orange Isle) and museums.

The user revealed the philosophical foundation of the training: each person has unique '出厂设置' (factory settings) leading to different '缘起' (perceived conditions/causes) and '执取' (instinctive attachments/clinging), using Buddhist epistemology to explain human cognitive diversity.

The user explicitly frames AI-powered self-assessment and trait profiling as modern '观照' (clear seeing/contemplation) tools that help people see their own '缘起' and '本能执取' (instinctive attachments), serving as a technological substitute for traditional introspective practices like meditation.

The user has productized '发现/发展喜欢与天赋' (discovering/developing passion and talent) based on 20+ years of organizational development practice and 8 years of dedicated research and validation, using AI technology.

The user's productization focuses on three key intervention points for college students: '科学选专业' (scientific major selection), '科学学习' (scientific learning), and '科学发展' (scientific career development) — designed to address the most confusing, difficult, and anxiety-provoking aspects of the college experience respectively.
The user values analysis that explicitly connects the training design to its philosophical and technological underpinnings, not just evaluating surface-level pedagogical structures.

The user's training framework treats individual differences not as 'bugs to be fixed' but as natural outcomes of different '缘起', fundamentally inverting the traditional educational logic of standardization.

The user revealed that the training program's three intervention points (major selection, learning methods, career development) serve as entry points for deeper self-exploration, each designed to trigger the fundamental question 'Who am I?'.

The assistant explicitly referenced the previous OCR hallucination error (sports analysis from a black image) as a lesson learned, stating a new policy: '在无法可靠确认图片视觉内容之前，绝不编造' (never fabricate before reliably confirming the visual content).

The assistant explicitly admitted it cannot directly read or parse PNG image content because its available toolset lacks image-reading tools (only write_todos, web_search, web_fetch, present_files, ask_clarification).
The assistant reiterated its no-hallucination policy established after the previous OCR error, stating: '在无法可靠确认图片视觉内容之前，绝不编造' (never fabricate before reliably confirming the visual content).
The user asked '你为什么不自己读取图片内容呢' (Why don't you read the image content yourself?), expressing direct skepticism about the assistant's technical limitations.

The assistant explicitly listed its available tools: write_todos, web_search (API key unavailable), web_fetch (does not support file:// protocol), present_files, and ask_clarification — confirming no image-reading or file-processing tools are available.
